query: PostgreSQL sub 100ms query latency 10TB dataset optimization partitioning TimescaleDB
answer: To achieve sub 100ms query latency on a 10TB dataset, use TimescaleDB's hypertables for automatic time-based partitioning and optimized indexing for time-series data.

results.title: 10 Strategies for Scaling High-Volume Data in Postgres - DEV Community
results.snippet: *Learn 10 practical PostgreSQL scaling strategies for high-volume data - from batch ingestion and strategic indexing to specialized extensions for time-series workloads with code examples.*. Let's explore practical scaling approaches you can implement today, with a special focus on time-series data handling. However, for high-velocity time-series data, even this approach might not be sufficient. While standard PostgreSQL partitioning provides benefits, it requires manual management and doesn't automatically optimize for time-series access patterns. ## Specialized Extensions for Time-Series Data. Hypertables automatically partition your time-series data into manageable chunks:. Unlike standard partitioning, this approach automatically handles time-based chunking and optimizes for typical time-series query patterns, significantly improving both ingest rates and query performance. TimescaleDB's time-indexed design facilitates column-level compression; it achieves impressive results for time-series data:. For truly massive workloads, Timescale offers multi-node capabilities to distribute your database across multiple machines while maintaining a single logical view of your data. PostgreSQL provides robust native tools for scaling, with specialized extensions available for specific use cases like time-series data.
